title: Oil Holds Gains, Bonds Drop on Inflation Concerns: Markets Wrap
summary: Oil held near its highest close since mid-June, weighing on stocks and Treasuries, as an escalation in Middle East hostilities rekindled worries about inflation.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
summary: Oil held a two-day gain as the US and Iran continued hostilities in the Middle East, while Yemen’s Houthi militants threatened to blockade Saudi Arabia’s maritime traffic in the Red Sea.

title: New Zealand Inflation Accelerates to 4.1% as Fuel Costs Soar
summary: New Zealand’s annual inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years in the second quarter, fanned by soaring fuel prices and reinforcing the Reserve Bank’s decision to begin raising interest rates.

title: Kioxia Bulls Ride Out Volatility With Targets 130% Above Market
summary: Analysts are maintaining bullish views on Kioxia Holdings Inc. despite a deepening share price correction, saying the stock is likely to resume its upward trajectory once supply-demand conditions improve.

title: LA’s $1.2 Billion ‘Graffiti Towers’ Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
summary: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more than seven years after the original Chinese developer ran out of money.

title: Gilts Rattled on Andy Burnham’s First Day as Fiscal Fears Return
summary: UK government bonds dropped after new Prime Minister Andy Burnham unnerved investors on his first day in the job over his approach to the country’s finances.

title: Netflix Taps US High-Grade Bond Market With $1 Billion Debt Sale
summary: Netflix Inc. raised $1 billion from a US high-grade bond sale on Monday, its first since a debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
summary: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility which further boosts their carry appeal.

title: Goldman Sells $2.5 Billion Preferreds Following Spread Low
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. priced new preferred shares Monday, just days after a metric determining risk and value in these securities hit its lowest level since the global financial crisis.

title: S&P Slips as Apple, Tesla Keep Broader Chipmaker Gains in Check
summary: The S&P 500 Index erased early gains to finish lower as a decline in Apple Inc. and Tesla Inc. offset advances elsewhere. Chipmakers rebounded ahead of a deluge of earnings reports that will test whether Big Tech’s profits can match high expectations.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
summary: Oil edged up in a fraught session as traders grappled with a barrage of headlines that suggest a worsening of the Middle East conflict, as well as attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

title: Stocks, Bonds Fall as US-Iran Risks Whipsaw Oil: Markets Wrap
summary: A renewed bout of oil volatility sent stocks lower, with concerns about an escalation in Middle East hostilities outweighing attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

title: DoubleLine Says Higher Bond Yields to Help Fed Keep Rates Steady
summary: DoubleLine is positioning in shorter-dated government bonds on the view that Kevin Warsh’s credibility with investors will help allow Federal Reserve officials to keep interest rates steady this year.

title: DC Readies $1.2 Billion Bond Sale as Credit Stabilizes Post-DOGE
summary: Washington, DC is stepping up to borrow $1.2 billion this week at its annual municipal debt sale, tapping the market as its credit stabilizes.

title: Canada Financials Stocks Close In on Top Spot as Energy Momentum Fades
summary: Canada’s financial stocks took a breather Monday after a torrid four-month rally that put them on the verge of overtaking energy as the best performing sector in the equity market this year.

title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rate Path Amid US-Iran Conflict
summary: Gold steadied after escalating US-Iran clashes over the weekend were followed by reports of mediation efforts, with traders assessing the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

title: Lionheart (CUB) Nears $400 Million Deal for Venezuela Oil Stake
summary: Lionheart Capital is close to a $400 million deal for a stake in a Venezuelan oil field, in what would be one of the first major private investments in the country’s oil industry since the US began easing sanctions.

title: US Immigration Policies Sink Mexican Grocery Chain Chedraui
summary: President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown is causing the worst rout on the Mexican stock exchange this year.

title: Money Funds Keep Cash Closer as Fed Leaves Markets Guessing
summary: Money‑market funds are shifting toward ultra short-term holdings and away from assets with even modest interest-rate risk as uncertainty grows over the Federal Reserve’s policy path and the near-term outlook for rates.

title: BlackRock Eyes More Than $12 Billion Debt for Data Center
summary: BlackRock Inc. is looking to sell more than $12 billion of bonds to help finance a Meta Platforms Inc. data center campus in El Paso, Texas, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, the latest in a string of debt deals fueling tech companies’ massive investments in artificial intelligence.
